list.title: Opening - Ouverture
list.description: Opening remarks for WTM Montreal 2026.

list.title: Protection de la vie privée dans les systèmes d'IA
list.description: The presentation will focus on privacy protection, covering existing threats to the privacy of machine learning models, training data and user inputs, as well as an overview of current countermeasures.

list.title: The cost of one-solution-fits-all models
list.description: This talk examines how the shift from highly customizable, small-scale machine learning systems to large, reusable models changes the AI landscape. As development moves toward off-the-shelf systems, the wide space of possible models narrows, and AI systems begin to look and behave more alike across contexts. The talk then asks what is gained and lost through this homogenization, and how responsibility should be understood when key design choices are no longer made locally, but embedded in widely deployed models.

list.title: Panel - IA : PROMESSES ET ANGLES MORTS
list.description: A deep dive into the promises and blind spots of Artificial Intelligence with industry experts.

list.title: Community Demo — AI Tinkerers Montreal
list.description: A rapid-fire series of live demos from the AI Tinkerers Montreal community. Featuring 'Supercharging Development with Gemini CLI' by Sabine Pierre and other cutting-edge AI experiments.

list.title: Closing Remarks
list.description: Final thank you and raffle prizes for WTM Montreal 2026.

list.title: POPPING THE HOOD: The Grime Behind the Genius
list.description:
Everyone is obsessed with the "shiny red car"—the AI Model. We chase parameters and context windows, fighting to get into the driver's seat. But nobody is asking the most dangerous question: "Is the track safe?" When you put a $15 million machine on a broken track, it doesn’t just stop—it crashes.

AI isn't magic; it's a high-maintenance machine. In this session, we pop the hood to reveal the grime behind the genius. We will leave the "driver obsession" behind and look at the infrastructure required to actually finish the race.

list.title: Build Your First AI Agent with Google ADK
list.description: Learn to build intelligent AI agents using Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK). In this hands-on workshop, you'll create a custom agent using free-tier resources. We'll walk through crafting agent instructions, understanding function-calling capabilities, testing locally with the playground, and exploring how agents reason and make decisions.

list.title: An incursion into the intriguing world of AI infrastructure
list.description: This presentation explores how a sustainable strategy based on the triple bottom line—water, waste, and energy—can guide the development of AI data centers. It highlights the rapid growth of AI in a context of limited resources, reveals the water and energy footprint of its lifecycle, and presents the main climate challenges and risks identified by the industry for Canada's data center sector.

list.title: The Human Edge: Soft Skills in the Age of AI
list.description: As artificial intelligence reshapes industries and automates routine tasks, leaders face a fundamental question: What kind of world of work do we want to create? For centuries, humans were expected to work like machines. Now, in the age of AI, humans can focus on what only they can do while machines handle the rest.

list.title: What Models Can See: Inside Modern Computer Vision
list.description: Discover how modern vision models can detect objects without ever being trained on them. In this hands-on workshop, we will explore the evolution from traditional object detection to zero-shot models like Grounding DINO.

list.title: Community Panel - PARCOURS INSPIRANTS ET LEADERSHIP
list.description: An inspiring panel discussion moderated by Reem Habib, featuring Ghislaine Boundjia and Nicole Kenmei from Black Ladies in Tech, and Mahaua Chowdhury from Deloitte. They will share their professional journeys, discuss untapped opportunities, and provide insights to help you navigate the tech landscape and build a strategic, authentic career path.

list.title: Community Panel - FEMMES EN TECH
list.description: A collaborative panel discussion featuring representatives from Technovation Montréal, Femmes+ En Tech, and Les Scientifines discussing the ecosystem for women in technology.

list.title: Atelier - L’ESPACE DES POSSIBLES: RÉSEAUTEZ AVEC CONFIANCE
list.description: Interactive workshop on networking with confidence, focusing on human-centric strategies and authenticity.

list.title: Atelier - BEYOND THE DASHBOARD: BUILDING AGENTS THAT ACTUALLY USE YOUR DATA
list.description: Workshop on moving beyond simple data visualization to building intelligent agents that leverage data for actionable insights.

list.title: Quand l’IA apprend à comparer : Biais, complexité et données structurées
list.description: Exploration of bias and complexity when AI learns to compare structured data.

list.title: Atelier - DANS LES COULISSES DU JEU VIDÉO : CRÉEZ VOTRE TOUT PREMIER JEU AVEC UNITY
list.description: Hands-on workshop on creating your first video game using the Unity game engine.

list.title: Atelier - LES DÉCISIONS QUI CHANGENT TOUT DANS UN PROJET NUMÉRIQUE
list.description: Workshop on critical decision-making in digital projects, focusing on value and strategic alignment.

list.title: Panel - THE TECH TALENT BARRIER FOR ENGLISH-SPEAKING WOMEN
list.description: An interactive data-driven session sharing documented barriers to tech employment for English-speaking women in Quebec. Industry leaders respond with concrete actionable solutions.

list.title: Punk strategies to find your voice!
list.description: Punk culture, in its political function, stands against the fundamentals of capitalism. Appropriation, hacktivism, and culture jamming are among the consistently effective strategies associated with subcultures.

list.title: Project Management Tools
list.description: Requirements management in a project goes far beyond simply defining SMART objectives. This presentation will offer a demonstration and contextualization of several simple tools for effectively defining, organizing, and managing requirements.

list.title: Beyond the Pixel
list.description: Let's create a dialogue between digital and physical communication media. Group exploration of imaginary digital tool designs meeting social needs.

list.title: Gamification in the service of science
list.description: Gamification and serious games are now powerful tools for making science more accessible, engaging, and understandable for both expert and non-specialist audiences.

list.title: From Theory to Practice: Reinforcement Learning in Video Games
list.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) is a powerful paradigm for training intelligent agents through interaction with their environment, yet its adoption in the video game industry remains limited.

list.title: Break It Before They Do - Threat Modeling at Startup Speed
list.description: The internet is adversarial by default and the threat landscape is changing day by day. In this talk, I'll present current security trends and some real-world breach examples of the technical threats they pose.

list.title: Prendre sa place dans un milieu traditionnellement masculin
list.description: Quelques outils pratiques et stratégies concrètes à utiliser afin de gagner en confiance, influence et légitimité. Vous serez plus en mesure de vous affirmer et de prendre votre place dans des environnements à dominance masculine et d’y évoluer avec plus d’assurance.
